title: Copy Files to Containers
description:
## Copy Files Between Containers and the Cluster

The `kubectl cp` command allows you to copy files and directories to and from running containers.

**Your task:**

A pod named `config-pod` is running with a file at `/src/config.json`. Copy it **into** the same pod at `/app/config.json` using `kubectl cp`.

> Tip: `kubectl cp` syntax is `kubectl cp <source> <pod>:<dest>`

hints.title: Use kubectl cp
hints.body: First copy the file out from /src, then copy it back to /app, or use kubectl exec to verify.
hints.command: kubectl cp config-pod:/src/config.json /tmp/config.json && kubectl cp /tmp/config.json config-pod:/app/config.json

setup_commands.command: kubectl exec config-pod -- sh -c 'mkdir -p /src && echo eyJzdGF0dXMiOiAib2sifQo= | base64 -d > /src/config.json'
